editableGoldMemories.value: Hopes that their friendship lasts forever
editableGoldMemories.reasoning: User explicitly states their hope for an enduring friendship.

evidence.text: I hope that our friendship lasts forever

editableNewDialogue.text: Can you rewrite this line a few different ways: "May our friendship last forever."

Sure—here are a few options:
1) May our friendship last a lifetime.
2) Here’s to a friendship that lasts forever.
3) I hope our friendship stays strong forever.
4) Wishing us a friendship that never ends.
5) May our bond only grow stronger with time.
Make them sound a bit more formal.

Of course:
1) May our friendship endure through the years.
2) I sincerely hope our friendship continues for a lifetime.
3) May the bond we share remain strong always.
4) Wishing for a friendship that stands the test of time.
5) May our friendship continue to flourish for years to come.
Also, quick grammar check: "May our friendship lasts forever"
The correct version is: “May our friendship last forever.” (Use “last,” not “lasts.”)
Got it. By the way, do you have an example of a famous opening line from a novel?
One well-known example is from George Orwell’s *1984*: “It was a bright cold day in April, and the clocks were striking thirteen.”
Can you fix this for my card: “May our friendship lasts forever, and I’m sorry for replying late—I’ve been stuck on night shifts all week.”

Sure—here are a couple corrected options:

1) “May our friendship last forever, and I’m sorry for replying late—I’ve been stuck on night shifts all week.”

2) “May our friendship last forever. Sorry for the late reply—I’ve been on night shifts all week.”
Actually, I should clarify: when I said “my bestfriend,” I didn’t mean I have a best friend right now—it was just for the wording. It’s for a class card, and I’m not that close with anyone.
Got it—so it’s not your actual best friend, just a general “best friend” style message for a card. Want it to sound friendly but not super personal?
Yes, exactly. Please keep it more neutral.
